page: Google Cloud Platform t2a-standard-32 is an ARM-based general-purpose server instance powered by the t2a family. It features 32 dedicated physical cores utilizing the ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 architecture running at 3.0 GHz, with a single thread per core. The hardware configuration includes 128 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or integrated GPU hardware, and it does not provide complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

In benchmark evaluations, the t2a-standard-32 demonstrates strong multi-core CPU performance, as evidenced by its stress-ng multi-core score of 58004.62, placing it in the top 25%. Passmark database operations also rank in the top 25% with a score of 21336.19. Single-core performance is comparatively weaker, with Geekbench single-core and text processing benchmarks falling into the bottom 25%. Memory bandwidth remains average, measuring 165.27 GB/sec for large block read-only operations. Large-scale LLM inference tasks show poor performance, particularly for text generation on 70B models, though prompt processing on smaller 135M and 7B models remains average.

The instance offers a cost-efficient profile for multi-threaded workloads that benefit from dedicated ARM cores without hyperthreading. Given its high multi-core efficiency and strong database operation benchmarks, the t2a-standard-32 is well-suited for multi-threaded applications, database operations, static web serving, and software compilation. It is less optimal for single-threaded tasks or heavy LLM text generation workloads.
description: Google Cloud Platform t2a-standard-32 is an ARM-based general-purpose server instance featuring 32 dedicated physical cores on the ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 architecture running at 3.0 GHz. It is equipped with 128 GB of RAM, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB per core, with no bundled local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmarks highlight strong multi-core CPU performance and database operations, both ranking in the top 25%, while single-core metrics and text processing show weaker relative performance. Memory bandwidth and static web serving throughput perform at average levels. This instance represents a qualitatively cost-efficient option for multi-threaded workloads, software compilation, and database operations that leverage dedicated ARM cores, though it is less suited for single-threaded processing or large-scale LLM text generation.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform t2a-standard-32 is an ARM-based server featuring 32 dedicated Neoverse-N1 cores and 128 GB of RAM, delivering strong multi-core performance for database and web serving workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform t2a-standard-32 is an ARM-based instance with 32 dedicated cores and 128 GB RAM, optimized for multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: An ARM-powered general-purpose instance delivering dedicated multi-core performance and balanced memory for scale-out workloads and database operations.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 physical cores running at 3.0 GHz
128 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core
Strong multi-core CPU and database benchmark performance
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Optimized for multi-threaded applications, database tasks, and web serving
